description: Production-ready infrastructure upgrade + 12 features. Read ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md FIRST.
prerequisites: Before starting ANY phase: Jeet must complete ALL steps in ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 2 (Infrastructure Setup). Verify email flow works, DKIM passes, Telnyx sends SMS, VPS firewall is configured.
security_rules_for_every_story: Every API endpoint MUST verify Supabase JWT via getServerSession or createRouteHandlerClient
Every database query MUST be scoped to auth.uid() — verify RLS policy exists
Every user input MUST be validated with zod schema before database insertion
Every webhook endpoint MUST verify provider signature before processing payload
Every new table MUST have RLS enabled with appropriate policies
Never store secrets in code — all from process.env
Never use AsyncStorage for tokens/keys — only expo-secure-store
Log all sensitive operations to audit_log table
Rate limit all endpoints per security spec in ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 10D
claude_code_instructions: For each story: 1) Read ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md for context. 2) Implement the code. 3) Write tests matching ALL acceptance criteria including security checks. 4) Run tests. 5) Set passes to true. 6) Commit with message format 'v2-XXX: title'. 7) Move to next story. If a story references a file path, that's the exact file to create/modify.

phases.name: Database Migrations + Security Foundation
phases.description: Create all new tables, RLS policies, audit logging, and rate limiting infrastructure before building any features.

stories.title: V2 database migrations — all new tables
stories.description: Create a single Supabase migration file containing ALL table definitions from ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 11. This includes: alias_domains, tracker_logs, leak_detections, company_privacy_scores, email_summaries, digest_batches, honeypot_triggers, deletion_requests, company_privacy_contacts, autopilot_scans, audit_log, families, family_members. Also ALTER identities table to add: domain_id, reverse_alias_token, reply_enabled, phone_provider, phone_provider_sid, is_honeypot, service_label, simplelogin_alias_id. Also ALTER user_settings to add: email_forward_mode, digest_frequency, digest_time, digest_day, autopilot_enabled, autopilot_auto_kill_days, nuke_contact_number.
acceptance: Migration file at supabase/migrations/YYYYMMDD_v2_tables.sql runs without errors
All 12 new tables created with correct column types and foreign keys
All identity and user_settings ALTER statements applied
RLS enabled on EVERY new table
RLS policies match ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 11 exactly
Indexes created on: tracker_logs(user_id, processed_at), email_summaries(user_id, received_at), audit_log(user_id, created_at)
audit_log has NO insert/update/delete policy for users — only service role can write

stories.title: Audit logging middleware
stories.description: Create lib/audit.ts — a utility that logs sensitive operations to the audit_log table. Must use Supabase service role client (not user client) since users have no write access to audit_log. Include: logAudit(userId, action, resourceType, resourceId, metadata, request). Extract IP from x-forwarded-for header, user-agent from request headers.
acceptance: lib/audit.ts exports logAudit function
Uses supabase service role client for insert (bypasses RLS)
Extracts IP address from x-forwarded-for or x-real-ip headers
Extracts user-agent from request headers
Handles errors gracefully — audit failure must NOT break the main request
Test: calling logAudit creates record in audit_log with all fields populated
Test: logAudit with invalid user_id still doesn't throw (logs warning instead)

stories.title: Rate limiting middleware upgrade
stories.description: Upgrade existing rate limiting from Phase 9 to support per-endpoint limits. Create lib/rate-limit.ts that uses Supabase or in-memory store (Vercel Edge compatible). Implement limits from ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 10D: API 100/min/user, webhooks 1000/min/IP, AI summarization 100/day/user, GDPR sending 10/day/user, nuke 1/24hr/user, alias creation 20/hr/user, phone purchase 5/day/user.
acceptance: lib/rate-limit.ts exports rateLimit function with configurable limits
Supports per-user limits (keyed on user_id from JWT)
Supports per-IP limits (for webhook endpoints)
Supports daily limits (GDPR, AI summary, nuke)
Returns 429 Too Many Requests with Retry-After header when exceeded
Test: rapid calls beyond limit return 429
Test: different users have independent counters
Logs rate limit hits to audit_log

stories.title: Webhook signature verification library
stories.description: Create lib/webhooks/verify.ts with functions: verifyTelnyxSignature(headers, body) using ed25519 verification with TELNYX_PUBLIC_KEY env var, verifySimpleLoginWebhook(headers, body) using HMAC-SHA256 with SIMPLELOGIN_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var, verifyCronSecret(headers) checking Authorization: Bearer CRON_SECRET.
acceptance: lib/webhooks/verify.ts exports all three verification functions
verifyTelnyxSignature uses ed25519 with telnyx-signature-ed25519 and telnyx-timestamp headers
verifyTelnyxSignature rejects requests older than 5 minutes (replay protection)
verifySimpleLoginWebhook uses HMAC-SHA256
verifyCronSecret compares timing-safe against CRON_SECRET env var
All functions return boolean (no exceptions thrown)
Test: valid signature returns true
Test: tampered body returns false
Test: missing headers returns false
Test: expired timestamp returns false

stories.title: Zod validation schemas for all v2 inputs
stories.description: Create lib/validations/v2-schemas.ts with zod schemas for all v2 user inputs: alias creation (label max 100 chars, alphanumeric+spaces+punctuation, strip HTML), company name (max 200 chars, sanitized), phone number (E.164: /^\+[1-9]\d{1,14}$/), GDPR request (company_name, company_email valid email), honeypot creation (label, planted_at_service, planted_at_url valid URL), digest settings (mode enum, frequency enum, time string), domain report (domain_id UUID).
acceptance: lib/validations/v2-schemas.ts exports named zod schemas for each input type
Alias label strips HTML tags before validation
Company name strips HTML tags before validation
Phone number validates E.164 format strictly
GDPR company_email validates email format
Honeypot planted_at_url validates URL format (optional field)
All schemas have .transform() to sanitize strings (trim, strip HTML)
Test: XSS attempt in label '<script>alert(1)</script>Netflix' → 'Netflix'
Test: SQL injection attempt in company name returns sanitized string
Test: invalid phone number '+abc' fails validation
Test: valid inputs pass all schemas

phases.name: Phone Provider Abstraction + Telnyx
phases.description: Build provider abstraction layer. Implement Telnyx. Refactor Twilio. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 4.

stories.title: Phone provider interface and factory
stories.description: Create lib/phone/provider.ts with PhoneProvider interface as defined in ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 4A. Create lib/phone/factory.ts with getPhoneProvider() that reads PHONE_PROVIDER env var. See exact interface definition in architecture doc.
acceptance: lib/phone/provider.ts exports PhoneProvider interface with: buyNumber, releaseNumber, sendSMS, verifyWebhook, parseInboundSMS
lib/phone/factory.ts exports getPhoneProvider() returning correct implementation based on PHONE_PROVIDER env var
Factory throws descriptive error for unknown provider value
Factory throws if PHONE_PROVIDER env var is not set
TypeScript types are strict — no 'any' types

stories.title: Telnyx provider implementation
stories.description: Create lib/phone/providers/telnyx.ts implementing PhoneProvider. Install telnyx npm package (https://www.npmjs.com/package/telnyx, repo: https://github.com/team-telnyx/telnyx-node). Use: client.numberOrders.create() for buying, client.messages.send() for SMS with messaging_profile_id from TELNYX_MESSAGING_PROFILE_ID env var. Webhook verification via ed25519 using TELNYX_PUBLIC_KEY. Parse inbound from data.payload structure.
acceptance: npm install telnyx added to package.json
lib/phone/providers/telnyx.ts implements all PhoneProvider methods
buyNumber uses client.numberOrders.create with phone_numbers array
sendSMS uses client.messages.send with messaging_profile_id from env
verifyWebhook checks ed25519 signature + timestamp freshness (5 min max)
parseInboundSMS extracts from data.payload.from.phone_number, data.payload.to[0].phone_number, data.payload.text
All env vars used: TELNYX_API_KEY, TELNYX_MESSAGING_PROFILE_ID, TELNYX_PUBLIC_KEY
Test: mock Telnyx client, verify buyNumber calls correct API
Test: mock Telnyx client, verify sendSMS includes messaging_profile_id
Test: webhook verification rejects tampered signatures

stories.title: Twilio provider refactor
stories.description: Refactor existing lib/twilio.ts into lib/phone/providers/twilio.ts implementing PhoneProvider interface. No new functionality — just interface compliance. Keep existing Twilio SDK usage but wrapped in the interface methods.
acceptance: lib/phone/providers/twilio.ts implements PhoneProvider interface
All existing Twilio functionality preserved
Old lib/twilio.ts replaced with re-export from new location for backwards compat
Test: existing Twilio tests still pass
Test: factory returns TwilioProvider when PHONE_PROVIDER=twilio

stories.title: Telnyx SMS webhook handler
stories.description: Create app/api/webhooks/telnyx-sms/route.ts. Must: 1) verify webhook signature using lib/webhooks/verify.ts, 2) parse inbound SMS using provider.parseInboundSMS, 3) query identities table by phone number to find owner, 4) send push notification with SMS content, 5) log SMS metadata, 6) log to audit_log, 7) rate limit by source IP.
acceptance: POST /api/webhooks/telnyx-sms endpoint exists
First action: verify signature — reject 401 if invalid
Rejects requests with timestamps older than 5 minutes
Looks up identity by phone number (handles E.164 format matching)
Sends push notification to identity owner with SMS sender + text
Returns 200 for valid webhook, 401 for bad signature, 404 for unknown number
Logs to audit_log: action='sms_received', resource_type='identity'
Rate limited: 1000/min per source IP
Test: valid webhook with known number → 200 + push notification triggered
Test: valid webhook with unknown number → 200 (no error, just ignore)
Test: invalid signature → 401
Does NOT expose any user data in response body

stories.title: Update identity creation/deletion to use phone provider abstraction
stories.description: Refactor identity creation and deletion flows to use getPhoneProvider() instead of direct Twilio calls. When creating identity with phone: call provider.buyNumber(), store provider name and provider_sid on identity. When killing identity: call provider.releaseNumber(). Update phone_provider column on identity.
acceptance: Identity creation uses getPhoneProvider().buyNumber()
Identity stores phone_provider and phone_provider_sid from provider response
Identity deletion uses getPhoneProvider().releaseNumber()
Works with both Telnyx and Twilio providers
Logs phone purchase/release to audit_log
Rate limited: phone purchase 5/day per user
Test: creating identity with phone calls buyNumber on correct provider
Test: killing identity with phone calls releaseNumber with correct provider_sid

phases.name: SimpleLogin Bridge + Multi-Domain
phases.description: Build the bridge between our app and SimpleLogin's database. Add multi-domain rotation. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Sections 3 and 5.

stories.title: SimpleLogin database bridge
stories.description: Create lib/email/alias-sync.ts. This connects to SimpleLogin's PostgreSQL (separate from Supabase) using SIMPLELOGIN_DB_URI env var. Implements: createAlias(userId, aliasEmail, forwardingEmail) — creates record in SimpleLogin's alias table + our identities table. deactivateAlias(aliasId) — sets enabled=false in SimpleLogin. Uses connection pooling. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3A.
acceptance: lib/email/alias-sync.ts connects to SimpleLogin Postgres via SIMPLELOGIN_DB_URI
Uses pg npm package with connection pooling (max 5 connections)
createAlias creates record in SimpleLogin's alias table with correct user_id, mailbox_id
createAlias stores simplelogin_alias_id on our identities record
deactivateAlias sets enabled=false on SimpleLogin's alias record
Connection errors handled gracefully with retry logic (3 attempts, exponential backoff)
Database credentials only in env vars, never logged
Test: mock pg client, verify correct SQL queries
Test: connection failure retries 3 times then throws

stories.title: Multi-domain management API
stories.description: Create API endpoints for domain management. GET /api/v2/domains — list active domains. POST /api/v2/domains/:id/report-blocked — report a domain as blocked (increments blocked_count, auto-deactivates at threshold of 10). Create lib/email/domains.ts with getActiveDomains() and selectDomainForNewAlias(userId). See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 5.
acceptance: GET /api/v2/domains returns list of active, verified domains (auth required)
POST /api/v2/domains/:id/report-blocked increments blocked_count (auth required)
Domain auto-deactivated when blocked_count >= 10
lib/email/domains.ts selectDomainForNewAlias spreads aliases across domains
Selection excludes: inactive domains, unverified domains, high-blocked-count domains
Selection prefers domains the user doesn't already have aliases on
Validated with zod: domain_id must be valid UUID
Rate limited: report-blocked 10/hour per user (prevent abuse)
Logged to audit_log when domain auto-deactivated
Test: user with aliases on domains A and B gets domain C for next alias
Test: all domains blocked returns error with clear message
Test: report-blocked at threshold 10 deactivates domain

stories.title: Update identity creation to use multi-domain + SimpleLogin
stories.description: Refactor identity creation: when creating email alias, 1) select domain via selectDomainForNewAlias, 2) generate random alias address, 3) create alias in SimpleLogin via alias-sync bridge, 4) store domain_id and simplelogin_alias_id on identity. When killing: deactivate via bridge.
acceptance: New email identities use selectDomainForNewAlias for domain selection
Alias format: randomword-randomchars@selected-domain.com
SimpleLogin alias created via bridge on identity creation
domain_id and simplelogin_alias_id stored on identity
Identity kill deactivates SimpleLogin alias via bridge
Alias creation rate limited: 20/hour per user
Logged to audit_log: 'identity_created' with domain info
Test: new identity creates SimpleLogin alias and stores IDs
Test: killed identity deactivates SimpleLogin alias

stories.title: Email events webhook handler
stories.description: Create app/api/webhooks/email-events/route.ts. SimpleLogin's job_runner sends events when emails are forwarded, bounced, etc. This endpoint: 1) verifies HMAC signature, 2) processes event type (forwarded, bounced, alias_disabled), 3) for forwards: creates tracker_log and email_summary records, triggers leak detection check, 4) for bounces: tracks bounce count, auto-disables after 3 hard bounces.
acceptance: POST /api/webhooks/email-events endpoint exists
Verifies HMAC-SHA256 signature using SIMPLELOGIN_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Rejects 401 on invalid signature
Handles 'forwarded' event: creates tracker_log record, checks for leak
Handles 'bounced' event: increments bounce count, auto-disables alias after 3 hard bounces
Handles 'alias_disabled' event: updates identity status, sends push notification
Rate limited: 1000/min per source IP
Does NOT expose any internal data in responses
Test: valid forwarded event creates tracker_log
Test: 3 hard bounces triggers auto-disable + push notification
Test: invalid signature returns 401

phases.name: Tracker Stripping + Leak Detection
phases.description: VPS-side tracker stripping service + app-side leak detection and dashboard. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3C and 6.

stories.title: Tracker stripper Docker service for VPS
stories.description: Create email-server/tracker-stripper/ directory with: main.py (Python SMTP proxy listening on port 20380, receives from Postfix, strips trackers, forwards to SimpleLogin on 20381), tracker_domains.json (merged blocklist with 200+ domains), Dockerfile, requirements.txt. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3C for architecture. Postfix config change: virtual_transport = smtp:127.0.0.1:20380 instead of 20381.
acceptance: email-server/tracker-stripper/main.py is a Python aiosmtpd server on port 20380
Receives email, parses HTML body, strips known tracker pixels
Removes 1x1 hidden images by size attributes and display:none style
Cleans UTM parameters from all href links (utm_source, utm_medium, utm_campaign, mc_eid, fbclid, gclid, etc.)
Adds X-PhantomShield-Trackers-Stripped header with count
Adds X-PhantomShield-Links-Cleaned header with count
Forwards cleaned email to SimpleLogin on port 20381
POSTs tracker stats to configurable webhook URL (our API)
tracker_domains.json contains 200+ known tracker domains
Dockerfile builds and runs the service
Handles emails without HTML body gracefully (just forward)
Handles malformed HTML without crashing
Does NOT modify plain text emails
Does NOT strip non-tracking images (width > 1 or height > 1)
Test: email with Mailchimp pixel → pixel removed, count = 1
Test: email with 3 UTM links → links cleaned, count = 3
Test: email with no trackers → forwarded unchanged, count = 0
Test: plain text email → forwarded unchanged

stories.title: Tracker stats API and dashboard data
stories.description: Create POST /api/webhooks/tracker-log (receives stats from VPS tracker stripper, secured by shared secret) and GET /api/v2/trackers/stats (returns user's 30-day stats). Stats include: total_trackers_blocked, total_links_cleaned, emails_processed, top_tracker_companies sorted by frequency, daily_trend array.
acceptance: POST /api/webhooks/tracker-log validates shared secret in Authorization header
Creates tracker_logs record with identity_id, user_id, counts, companies
GET /api/v2/trackers/stats requires auth, returns 30-day rolling stats
Stats response includes: total_trackers_blocked, total_links_cleaned, emails_processed
Includes top_tracker_companies array sorted by frequency (top 10)
Includes daily_trend array (last 30 days, {date, trackers, emails})
Empty state handled: returns zeros, not errors
Test: POST with valid secret creates record
Test: POST with invalid secret returns 401
Test: GET returns correct aggregated stats for test data

stories.title: Tracker dashboard mobile screen
stories.description: New card on home screen: shield icon with 'X Trackers Blocked This Month'. Tap to expand to full tracker dashboard. Dashboard shows: big number (total blocked), company breakdown (horizontal bar list), trend sparkline (last 30 days), per-alias breakdown. Pull to refresh.
acceptance: Home screen shows tracker blocked count card
Card loads data from GET /api/v2/trackers/stats
Tapping card navigates to full TrackerDashboard screen
Dashboard shows total blocked as large styled number
Shows top 5 tracker companies with bar visualization
Shows 30-day trend as sparkline or simple line
Shows per-alias breakdown (which aliases get most trackers)
Pull-to-refresh works
Loading skeleton shown while data fetches
Empty state: 'No trackers blocked yet. As you use your aliases, we'll show you who's tracking you.'
Accessible: screen reader labels on all elements

stories.title: Leak detection engine
stories.description: In the email events webhook handler (v2-014), add leak detection logic. When a forwarded email arrives: check if the identity has a service_label. If yes, compare sender domain against the label. If sender domain does NOT match the service_label (fuzzy match — netflix.com matches 'Netflix'), create leak_detection record and send push notification. Create GET /api/v2/leaks and PATCH /api/v2/leaks/:id/dismiss endpoints.
acceptance: Leak detection runs on every forwarded email for identities with service_label
Domain matching is fuzzy: 'Netflix' label matches netflix.com, email.netflix.com, etc.
Non-matching sender creates leak_detection record
Push notification: 'Your {service} alias received email from {sender_domain}'
GET /api/v2/leaks returns user's non-dismissed detections, sorted by date desc
PATCH /api/v2/leaks/:id/dismiss sets dismissed=true (auth + ownership verified)
Does NOT flag: emails from common transactional senders (sendgrid.net, mailgun.org, amazonaws.com) — these are infrastructure, not the company itself
Test: email from vitamins.com to Netflix-labeled alias → leak detected
Test: email from netflix.com to Netflix-labeled alias → no leak
Test: email from email.netflix.com to Netflix-labeled alias → no leak
Test: dismiss endpoint only works for own leak detections

stories.title: Leak alerts mobile screen
stories.description: New section in notifications or dedicated screen. Shows leak alerts with: identity label, unexpected sender domain, date, action buttons (Dismiss, Send Deletion Request, Kill Alias). Badge count on tab for unread alerts. Push notification deep links to this screen.
acceptance: Leak alerts screen lists non-dismissed leak detections
Each item shows: service name, unexpected sender, date
Dismiss button calls PATCH /api/v2/leaks/:id/dismiss
Send Deletion Request button navigates to GDPR flow pre-filled with company
Kill Alias button triggers identity kill with confirmation dialog
Badge count shows number of non-dismissed leaks
Push notification for new leak deep links to this screen
Empty state: 'No suspicious activity detected. Your aliases are clean.'
Pull to refresh

phases.name: AI Email Summaries + Smart Digest
phases.description: Email classification, AI summaries, digest batching. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3D-3E.

stories.title: Email classification and AI summary service
stories.description: Create lib/email/classifier.ts — classifies emails as transactional or marketing. Heuristic first (keyword matching), AI fallback via gpt-4o-mini. Create lib/email/summarizer.ts — generates one-line summary for marketing emails. Create POST /api/v2/email/summarize endpoint called by tracker stripper. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3D.
acceptance: lib/email/classifier.ts classifyEmail(subject, from, bodyPreview) returns 'transactional' | 'marketing'
Heuristic catches obvious cases without API call (receipt, password reset = transactional; unsubscribe, newsletter = marketing)
AI fallback uses gpt-4o-mini with max_tokens: 10, costs ~$0.0001 per call
lib/email/summarizer.ts summarizeEmail(subject, bodyText) returns one-line summary ≤ 20 words
POST /api/v2/email/summarize receives email metadata, returns {type, summary}
Endpoint secured by shared secret (called from VPS, not users)
AI summarization rate limited: 100/day per user
Email body truncated to 2000 chars before sending to OpenAI (cost control + security)
No PII from email body logged or stored in plain text
OPENAI_API_KEY from env, never exposed
Test: 'Your order has shipped' classified as transactional without AI call
Test: newsletter with 'unsubscribe' link classified as marketing without AI call
Test: ambiguous email calls AI and returns classification
Test: summary is ≤ 20 words

stories.title: Digest batching engine and cron
stories.description: Create POST /api/cron/digest endpoint. Runs every hour via Vercel Cron. For each user with email_forward_mode='digest': check if current time matches their digest_time. If yes, compile all email_summaries with forwarded=false and digest_batch_id IS NULL. Create digest_batch record. Format digest email with all summaries. Mark emails as forwarded. Update ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 3E.
acceptance: POST /api/cron/digest endpoint verifies CRON_SECRET
Finds users where email_forward_mode='digest' and current hour matches digest_time
Creates digest_batch record
Compiles all unforwarded marketing email summaries for each user
Formats digest as clean HTML email: one line per email with subject + AI summary
Sends digest via SimpleLogin's outgoing SMTP (not direct — goes through proper DKIM signing)
Marks all compiled emails as forwarded=true with digest_batch_id set
Handles users with no pending emails gracefully (skip, don't send empty digest)
Weekly mode: only sends on user's digest_day
Test: user with 5 unforwarded marketing emails gets digest with 5 summaries
Test: user with 0 unforwarded emails skipped
Test: cron rejects requests without valid CRON_SECRET

stories.title: Digest settings UI
stories.description: Settings sub-screen for email forwarding preferences. Toggle between: Forward All / Summaries Only / Daily Digest / Weekly Digest. Time picker for daily digest. Day + time picker for weekly. Save via PATCH /api/v2/settings/digest.
acceptance: Settings screen shows current email forward mode
Can toggle between: full, summary, digest modes
Daily mode shows time picker
Weekly mode shows day picker + time picker
Save button calls PATCH /api/v2/settings/digest
Input validated with zod schema before API call
Changes saved optimistically with rollback on error
Explanation text for each mode so user understands what happens

stories.title: In-app email viewer
stories.description: When user taps an email summary, open full email view. Decrypt full_body_encrypted from email_summaries table. Render in sandboxed WebView with JavaScript disabled. Show tracker count, leak status. Action buttons: Reply via Alias, Delete. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 10H for WebView security config.
acceptance: Tapping email summary navigates to EmailViewer screen
Decrypts full_body_encrypted using user's encryption key from expo-secure-store
Renders HTML in WebView with: javaScriptEnabled=false, allowFileAccess=false, mixedContentMode='never'
External links open in system browser (Linking.openURL), not in WebView
Images NOT loaded by default — 'Load Images' button to enable
Shows banner: 'X trackers were stripped from this email'
Shows leak status if applicable
Reply button navigates to compose (uses reverse alias from SimpleLogin)
Delete button removes email_summary record
Requires biometric to view (contains decrypted email content)
Works offline for cached/decrypted emails

phases.name: Honeypot Aliases + GDPR Automation
phases.description: Decoy aliases for detecting data selling + automated deletion requests. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Section 6-9.

stories.title: Honeypot alias creation and trigger detection
stories.description: POST /api/v2/honeypots — creates identity with is_honeypot=true. GET /api/v2/honeypots — lists honeypots with trigger data from honeypot_triggers table. In email events webhook (v2-014), add honeypot check: if receiving identity is_honeypot, do NOT forward email, instead create honeypot_triggers record and send high-priority push notification.
acceptance: POST /api/v2/honeypots creates identity with is_honeypot=true, service_label from request
Input validated: label required, max 100 chars, sanitized
GET /api/v2/honeypots returns honeypots with: label, planted_at_service, trigger_count, last_trigger
Email to honeypot alias: NOT forwarded to user
Honeypot trigger creates honeypot_triggers record with sender details
Push notification: high priority, '🚨 HONEYPOT TRIGGERED: Your alias planted at {service} received email from {sender}'
Rate limited: honeypot creation 5/day per user
Logged to audit_log: 'honeypot_created', 'honeypot_triggered'
Test: email to honeypot → not forwarded, trigger recorded, push sent
Test: email to non-honeypot → forwarded normally
Test: GET returns correct trigger counts

stories.title: Honeypot manager mobile screen
stories.description: Screen accessible from identities tab. Lists all honeypots with status: green checkmark = never triggered, red alert = triggered with count. Create new honeypot flow. Tap for trigger history detail view.
acceptance: Honeypot list shows all honeypots sorted by triggered status (triggered first)
Green badge: 'Clean' for never-triggered honeypots
Red badge: 'Triggered (N times)' with trigger count
Create flow: enter service label, optional description of where you'll plant it
Detail view shows full trigger history: date, sender email, sender domain
Delete button with confirmation dialog
Empty state: explains what honeypots are and how to use them
Pull to refresh

stories.title: GDPR/CCPA deletion request engine
stories.description: Create lib/gdpr/templates.ts with legally valid GDPR Article 17 and CCPA Section 1798.105 email templates. Create POST /api/v2/deletion-requests — generates email from template, sends FROM alias via SimpleLogin reverse-alias system, creates deletion_requests record with 30-day deadline. GET /api/v2/deletion-requests lists user's requests. PATCH to update status.
acceptance: lib/gdpr/templates.ts exports GDPR and CCPA email templates
Templates use ONLY alias email and company name as variables (no template injection possible)
Template text is legally sound (references correct articles/sections)
POST /api/v2/deletion-requests: validates input, generates email, sends via SimpleLogin reverse-alias
Email sent FROM the user's alias (company sees alias, not real email)
Creates deletion_requests record with status='sent', response_deadline=sent_at + 30 days
GET /api/v2/deletion-requests returns user's requests sorted by date desc
PATCH /api/v2/deletion-requests/:id allows updating status (completed, ignored, escalated)
Rate limited: 10 requests/day per user
Logged to audit_log: 'gdpr_request_sent'
Test: creating request sends email with correct template
Test: response_deadline is exactly 30 days after sent_at
Test: can't update another user's request (RLS enforced)

stories.title: Company privacy contacts database + API
stories.description: Seed company_privacy_contacts table with top 200 companies and their privacy@ / dpo@ email addresses. Create GET /api/v2/company-contacts/:domain — returns known contact or null. When user creates GDPR request for unknown company, save the contact they provide (crowdsource).
acceptance: Seed migration inserts 200+ company privacy contacts
Contacts include: Google, Apple, Amazon, Meta, Netflix, Uber, etc.
GET /api/v2/company-contacts/:domain returns contact if found, 404 if not
When GDPR request created for unknown company, user's provided email saved to company_privacy_contacts
contributed_by_count incremented for crowdsourced contacts
Bounced email addresses flagged as verified=false
Domain input validated and normalized (lowercase, trim)
Test: known company returns contact
Test: unknown company returns 404
Test: creating GDPR request for unknown company saves contact

stories.title: GDPR request tracker mobile screen
stories.description: Screen showing all user's deletion requests. Status badges: Sent (blue), Awaiting (yellow with countdown), Completed (green), Ignored (red, with escalation option). One-tap create from any identity. Mark complete and escalate actions.
acceptance: Request list shows all requests sorted by date desc
Status badges with correct colors per status
Awaiting status shows countdown: 'X days remaining' (30 - days since sent)
Create from identity: pre-fills company name from service_label, searches company_privacy_contacts
Mark Complete button updates status to 'completed'
Escalate button shows links to: FTC complaint form (US), relevant DPA (EU)
Overdue requests (past 30 days, still 'awaiting') highlighted in red
Pull to refresh

phases.name: Privacy Autopilot + Company Scores
phases.description: Automatic stale identity cleanup + aggregated company privacy scoring.

stories.title: Privacy autopilot scan engine
stories.description: Create POST /api/v2/autopilot/scan — scans user's identities for: aliases with 0 emails in 90 days, aliases where 100% of emails are spam, phone numbers with 0 SMS in 60 days. Stores results in autopilot_scans. GET /api/v2/autopilot/results returns latest scan. POST /api/v2/autopilot/kill bulk-kills stale identities from scan.
acceptance: POST /api/v2/autopilot/scan runs scan for authenticated user
Identifies stale aliases (no email_summaries in 90 days)
Identifies spam-only aliases (all emails from non-matching senders)
Identifies unused phone numbers (no SMS in 60 days)
Creates autopilot_scans record with counts
GET /api/v2/autopilot/results returns latest scan with list of stale identity IDs + reasons
POST /api/v2/autopilot/kill accepts array of identity IDs to kill (must be from latest scan)
Kill verifies each identity belongs to the user (RLS)
Kill deactivates SimpleLogin alias + releases phone number if applicable
Logged to audit_log: 'autopilot_scan', 'autopilot_kill'
Test: identity with no emails in 90 days flagged as stale
Test: identity with recent emails NOT flagged
Test: kill removes identities and releases resources

stories.title: Autopilot monthly cron and notifications
stories.description: Create POST /api/cron/autopilot — runs monthly for all users with autopilot_enabled=true. For auto-kill mode: automatically kill aliases meeting threshold. For manual mode: send push notification with count. Deep link to review screen.
acceptance: POST /api/cron/autopilot verifies CRON_SECRET
Runs scan for each user with autopilot_enabled=true
Manual mode: sends push '🧹 Privacy Cleanup: N stale identities found. Review?'
Auto-kill mode: kills aliases meeting autopilot_auto_kill_days threshold
Auto-kill sends summary push: 'Auto-killed N stale aliases this month'
Push notification deep links to autopilot review screen
Handles large user base: processes in batches of 100
Test: user with manual mode gets notification, no aliases killed
Test: user with auto-kill mode gets aliases killed + summary notification

stories.title: Autopilot settings and review UI
stories.description: Settings screen: toggle autopilot on/off, choose manual vs auto-kill mode, configure auto-kill threshold (30/60/90 days). Review screen: shows stale identities with reason, one-tap Kill or Keep per identity. After review shows 'Reduced footprint by X%'.
acceptance: Settings toggle for autopilot enabled/disabled
Mode selector: Manual Review / Auto-Kill
Auto-kill threshold picker: 30, 60, 90 days
Review screen shows each stale identity with: name, reason, last activity date
Kill button kills individual identity with confirmation
Keep button excludes from future scans (marks as reviewed)
After reviewing all: shows footprint reduction percentage
Trigger manual scan button on settings screen

stories.title: Company privacy scores computation and API
stories.description: Create POST /api/cron/company-scores — nightly job that aggregates leak_detections across all users (anonymized) to compute privacy scores per company domain. Score formula: starts at 100, loses points based on leak_rate, adjusted for sample size. GET /api/v2/company-scores/:domain — public endpoint (rate limited, no auth required).
acceptance: POST /api/cron/company-scores verifies CRON_SECRET
Aggregates: for each company domain, count total identities with that service_label, count leak_detections
Computes leak_rate = leak_detections / total_identities
Privacy score = 100 - (leak_rate * 100), clamped 0-100
Companies with < 10 identities: score = null (insufficient data)
Stores results in company_privacy_scores table
GET /api/v2/company-scores/:domain returns score, leak_rate, total_aliases, last_computed_at
Public endpoint — no auth required but rate limited: 60/min per IP
Returns 'insufficient_data' for companies below threshold
Response cached (Cache-Control: max-age=86400)
No individual user data exposed in response (only aggregates)
Test: company with 100 aliases and 5 leaks → score 95
Test: company with 3 aliases → insufficient_data

stories.title: Privacy score badge in alias creation and detail
stories.description: When creating alias and user enters service name, debounced lookup to GET /api/v2/company-scores/:domain. Show inline badge: 🟢 80-100 Low Risk, 🟡 50-79 Medium Risk, 🔴 0-49 High Risk. Also show on identity detail screen.
acceptance: Alias creation form: after typing service name, debounced (500ms) score lookup
Badge shows with correct color/risk level based on score
Shows 'No data available' for unknown companies (not an error state)
Identity detail screen shows score badge for labeled identities
Score refreshes on pull-to-refresh
Does not block alias creation if score lookup fails

phases.name: Emergency Nuke + Export + Family Plan
phases.description: Safety features, data portability, household plans. See ARCHITECTURE-V2-FINAL.md Sections 6-9.

stories.title: Emergency nuke API endpoint
stories.description: Create POST /api/v2/nuke. Requires biometric token from expo-secure-store sent in request header. Procedure: 1) kill all active identities (SimpleLogin + phone provider), 2) fire GDPR deletion emails for all labeled services with known contacts, 3) soft-delete account (recoverable 30 days with master password), 4) return success. Rate limited: 1 per 24 hours.
acceptance: POST /api/v2/nuke requires auth + biometric token in X-Biometric-Token header
Kills ALL active identities for user (SimpleLogin deactivate + phone release)
Sends GDPR deletion emails for identities with service_label AND known company contacts
Soft-deletes user account (sets deleted_at timestamp, account recoverable 30 days)
Does NOT delete audit_log records (retained for security)
Rate limited: 1 per 24 hours per user
Logged to audit_log: 'emergency_nuke' with counts of killed identities, GDPR emails sent
Returns: { identities_killed, gdpr_emails_sent, recovery_deadline }
Runs as atomic transaction — if any step fails, rolls back
Test: nuke kills all identities and sends GDPR emails
Test: nuke without biometric token returns 403
Test: second nuke within 24 hours returns 429

stories.title: Emergency nuke mobile UI
stories.description: Settings screen: 'Emergency' section with red styling. Big red 'NUKE EVERYTHING' button. Flow: tap → biometric prompt → confirmation dialog listing what will happen → optional 'I'm safe' message to emergency contact → final confirm → loading → success screen with recovery info.
acceptance: Red-styled Emergency section in settings
Tap triggers biometric authentication (FaceID/TouchID/PIN)
Confirmation dialog lists: 'This will kill X aliases, release Y phone numbers, send Z deletion requests'
Counts fetched from API before showing dialog
'I'm safe' toggle: if enabled, sends pre-configured message to nuke_contact_number
Final confirmation requires typing 'NUKE' (prevents accidental taps)
Loading screen during nuke with progress indicator
Success screen shows: what was done + 'Account recoverable until {date}'
App resets to clean/logged-out state after nuke
Emergency contact configurable in settings (stored in expo-secure-store)

stories.title: Portable data export
stories.description: Create GET /api/v2/export. Requires auth + biometric token. Returns JSON with: all identities (alias, service_label, created, email_count, status), tracker stats, leak detections, GDPR request history. Include SimpleLogin-compatible format for migration. Also CSV format via ?format=csv query param.
acceptance: GET /api/v2/export requires auth + X-Biometric-Token header
Returns comprehensive JSON with all user data
Includes identities with: alias email, service label, domain, created date, status, email count
Includes tracker stats summary
Includes leak detection history
Includes GDPR request history
Includes SimpleLogin-compatible alias import format
?format=csv returns CSV download
Content-Disposition header set for file download
Available on ALL tiers (including free)
Logged to audit_log: 'data_exported'
Does NOT include: encryption keys, master password hash, biometric data
Test: export contains all user's identities
Test: export without biometric token returns 403
Test: CSV format is valid and parseable

stories.title: Export UI in settings
stories.description: Settings screen: 'Export My Data' section. Buttons: Export as JSON, Export as CSV. Show estimated file size. Biometric required. Download via native Share sheet (React Native Share API).
acceptance: Export section in settings with two buttons
Biometric prompt before export
Loading indicator during generation
File shared via React Native Share API (native share sheet)
JSON file readable in any text editor
CSV file opens in spreadsheet apps
Error handling for large exports (timeout after 30s with retry option)

stories.title: Family plan API
stories.description: Create endpoints: POST /api/v2/family (create family, user becomes owner), POST /api/v2/family/invite (send invite email to family member), GET /api/v2/family/stats (aggregate stats across family — total trackers blocked, total aliases, total leaks). DELETE /api/v2/family/members/:id (owner only). Each family member has independent encrypted vault — owner CANNOT see members' aliases.
acceptance: POST /api/v2/family creates family with authenticated user as owner
Max 5 members per family (configurable)
POST /api/v2/family/invite sends email invite, creates pending family_members record
Invite requires valid email format, rate limited: 10/day per user
GET /api/v2/family/stats returns ONLY aggregate numbers (total trackers, aliases, leaks)
Stats NEVER expose individual member's aliases, emails, or identity details
DELETE /api/v2/family/members/:id only works for family owner
Owner cannot delete themselves (must delete entire family)
RLS ensures members can only see family they belong to
Logged to audit_log: 'family_created', 'family_invite_sent', 'family_member_removed'
Test: owner can invite and remove members
Test: member cannot remove other members
Test: stats only show aggregates, no individual data

Member list shows: name, role, joined date
